dataset: 40 CFR Part 141 - National Primary Drinking Water Regulations

pi_items.source: 40 CFR Part 141 contains National Primary Drinking Water Regulations
40 CFR § 141.62 specifies arsenic MCL of 0.010 mg/L (verified via Cornell Law and EPA sources)
Arsenic is listed in 40 CFR § 141.62 (Maximum contaminant levels for inorganic contaminants)
40 CFR § 141.62 specifies nitrate (as Nitrogen) MCL of 10 mg/L (verified via Cornell Law and EPA sources)
Nitrate is listed in 40 CFR § 141.62 (Maximum contaminant levels for inorganic contaminants)
40 CFR § 141.64 specifies TTHM MCL of 0.080 mg/L (verified via Cornell Law and EPA sources)
Total trihalomethanes (TTHM) is listed in 40 CFR § 141.64 (Maximum contaminant levels for disinfection byproducts)

Specified in task.json as the bookmark folder name to create

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims have been independently verified. The task requires researching 40 CFR Part 141 (National Primary Drinking Water Regulations) on ecfr.gov to find Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) for three contaminants: Arsenic (0.010 mg/L, 40 CFR § 141.62), Nitrate as Nitrogen (10.0 mg/L, 40 CFR § 141.62), and Total Trihalomethanes/TTHM (0.080 mg/L, 40 CFR § 141.64). These are authentic EPA regulations accessible at ecfr.gov. The data source is real and public (non-synthetic). The task requires agents to locate these regulatory values, create a JSON reference file, and bookmark relevant sections.
